free_text: Spontaneously fissioning isomers in U, Np, Pu and
Thin targets, ~ 10 mu-g/cm2, with good uniformity and
stability during long time irradiation with intense
beams were used. Targets are produced by vacuum
deposition onto nickel and carbon backings from
a tungsten crucible heated by electron bombardment.
The target thicknesses, 5-30 mu-g/cm2, are determined
by alpha-counting in a windowless gas-flow proportional
counter with 2Pi geometry. The thicknesses are also
determined from the prompt fission rates in 13 MeV p
and d irradiations, using calculated cross sections.
counter, recording prompt fission, serves as a monitor
Two annular semiconductor counters were used in
1. detection of fission-in-flight from a collimated
beam of recoils by means of large plastic detectors
2. delayed coincidences between protons and fission
fragments from a (d,p) process are recorded, using two
annular semiconductor counters and a recoil catcher.
Uncertainties in cross sections are estimated
